answer:
The following works in the corpus contain explicit references to "Sufi" figures or the "Sufi" persona, as well as the term *tasawwuf* (the Arabic word for Sufism), which qualifies as specifically Sufi or Sufi-adjacent (e.g., mystical purification of the heart, esoteric knowledge). These occur in the context of Urdu poetry, where Sufism heavily influenced themes of spiritual ecstasy (*rindi*), asceticism (*zuhd*), annihilation of the self (*fana*), and wandering mendicants (*dervish*). No other works in the corpus yielded such targeted references after extensive searches for key terms like "Sufi," "Sufism," *tasawwuf*, "dhikr," "samaʿ," "dervish," "fakir," "qalandar," "rindi," "zuhd," "fana," and "mast."[^1][^2][^3][^4]

**Bang-E-Dara Part 1** (Allama Iqbal): Multiple references to Sufis, *tasawwuf*, *zuhd* (asceticism), and *rindi* (spiritual intoxication), including a satirical portrait of a pseudo-Sufi cleric.[^5][^6]

**Bang-E-Dara Part 2** (Allama Iqbal): Direct reference to a Sufi discovering divine presence in the heart.[^7]

**Diwan-E-Ghalib** (Mirza Ghalib): Reference to *masa'il-e-tasawwuf* (issues/questions of Sufism) as esoteric knowledge contrasted with worldly vice.[^8] Additional Sufi-adjacent motifs like *fana* (self-annihilation in the divine) and *dervish* (wandering mystic) appear throughout.[^9]

**Diwan-E-Zauq** (Sheikh Ibrahim Zauq): References to Sufis and *ilm-e-tasawwuf* (knowledge of Sufism) as requiring heart-purification, plus *dervish* as the ideal mystic.[^10][^11]

**Bang-E-Dara Part 3** (Allama Iqbal): Sufi-adjacent *dervish* figure and *fana* motifs.[^12]

No dedicated treatises on Sufi practices (e.g., no systematic discussions of *dhikr* remembrance, *samaʿ* listening assemblies, or orders like Naqshbandi/Chishti) appear in the corpus.

[^1]: *Bang-E-Dara Part 1*, ch. 44 [*Zuhd aur Rindi*](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/44-zuhd-aur-rindi/translation/#p-52b34d).
[^2]: *Diwan-E-Zauq*, ch. 49 [*Voh kaun hai jo mujh pe ta'assuf nahin karta*](/works/sheikh-ibrahim-zauq-diwan-e-zauq-0fa798/49-voh-kaun-hai-jo-mujh-pe-taassuf-nahin-karta/translation/#p-655b56).
[^3]: *Bang-E-Dara Part 1*, ch. 44 [*Zuhd aur Rindi*](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/44-zuhd-aur-rindi/transliteration/#p-00d472).
[^4]: *Diwan-E-Ghalib*, ch. 204 [*Yeh na thi hamari qismat ke visaal-e-yaar hota*](/works/mirza-ghalib-diwan-e-ghalib-74ed4c/204-yeh-na-thi-hamari-qismat-ke-visaal-e-yaar-hota/transliteration/).
[^5]: *Bang-E-Dara Part 1*, ch. 44 [*Zuhd aur Rindi*](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/44-zuhd-aur-rindi/translation/#p-52b34d).
[^6]: *Bang-E-Dara Part 1*, ch. 44 [*Zuhd aur Rindi*](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/44-zuhd-aur-rindi/transliteration/#p-00d472).
[^7]: *Bang-E-Dara Part 2*, ch. 20 [*Salimi*](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-2-9bc58c/20-salimi/translation/#p-e4b1ba).
[^8]: *Diwan-E-Ghalib*, ch. 204 [*Yeh na thi hamari qismat ke visaal-e-yaar hota*](/works/mirza-ghalib-diwan-e-ghalib-74ed4c/204-yeh-na-thi-hamari-qismat-ke-visaal-e-yaar-hota/transliteration/).
[^9]: *Diwan-E-Ghalib*, ch. 36 [*Dil-e-naadaan tujhe hua kya hai*](/works/mirza-ghalib-diwan-e-ghalib-74ed4c/36-dil-e-naadaan-tujhe-hua-kya-hai/translation/#p-70f710); ch. 86 [*Ishrat-e-qatra hai darya mein fana ho jaana*](/works/mirza-ghalib-diwan-e-ghalib-74ed4c/86-ishrat-e-qatra-hai-darya-mein-fana-ho-jaana/transliteration/#p-89e76b).
[^10]: *Diwan-E-Zauq*, ch. 49 [*Voh kaun hai jo mujh pe ta'assuf nahin karta*](/works/sheikh-ibrahim-zauq-diwan-e-zauq-0fa798/49-voh-kaun-hai-jo-mujh-pe-taassuf-nahin-karta/translation/#p-655b56).
[^11]: *Diwan-E-Zauq*, ch. 25 [*Kab haq-parast zahid-e-jannat-parast hai*](/works/sheikh-ibrahim-zauq-diwan-e-zauq-0fa798/25-kab-haq-parast-zahid-e-jannat-parast-hai/translation/#p-e44bc0).
[^12]: *Bang-E-Dara Part 3*, ch. 4 [*Dil se jo baat nikalti hai asar rakhti hai*](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/04-dil-se-jo-baat-nikalti-hai-asar-rakhti-hai/translation/#p-819a53).
